qa.user_verbatim_complaint: In cycle dev-20260519-211515, dev claimed 17 modified + 8 created files that were ALL already present in git HEAD from prior session commits (6d28883, 28a1e85, 4d9f9f5, 6537d7d). Dev performed work-tree inspection rather than diffing against a pre-dispatch baseline.
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: All four agent files were modified with provenance contract language. The core mechanism (baseline capture + git diff derivation mandate + QA FAIL gate + changelog-analyst filter) is present and functional.
qa.overall_assessment: The four-part provenance contract is substantially implemented and passes all 5 pytest acceptance tests. However, there are two blocking issues: (1) the BA spec M5 literal text mandates ..HEAD diff form for changelog-analyst but the implementation correctly uses working-tree form — this is spec_text_vs_execution_drift that BA must correct; (2) the QA provenance check (AC3/M4) only covers dev.files_modified but not dev.files_created, leaving newly-created untracked files outside the provenance enforcement perimeter. Additionally, the AC4 test is under-enforcing: it accepts any diff --name-only text including the (wrong) ..HEAD form. Verdict: fail — BA spec M5 must be updated to match the correct implementation, and AC3 scope must be extended to files_created.

success_criteria_results.details: baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot appear in the Step 8 dispatch payload body (lines 678-679), not just as comments. The pre-dispatch capture block uses git rev-parse HEAD and git status --porcelain.

success_criteria_results.details: All three required elements present: git-diff derivation mandate, observed_preexisting[] field, baseline_head_sha as top-level field. Also baseline_dirty_snapshot was added as top-level field (codex fix incorporated by dev).

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md add a check: if dev.files_modified lists a path not in git diff vs baseline, raise FAIL with primary_cause = 'dev_implementation'

Read agents/changelog-analyst.md lines 117-128; confirmed provenance_anomaly classification and enrichment exclusion. pytest tests AC3 and AC4 pass.
success_criteria_results.details: QA provenance check (agents/qa.md:412-414) only covers dev.files_modified, not dev.files_created. The requirement source phrase says 'files_modified lists a path' but M4 specifies 'dev.files_modified' only. However, the context ticket scope and changelog-analyst.md:125 both apply the filter to files_created too. QA spec is narrower than intended. This is a gap in AC3/M4 enforcement scope. Additionally, the BA spec M5 literal text mandates ..HEAD form for changelog-analyst but the implementation uses working-tree form — see spec_text_vs_execution_drift finding below.
success_criteria_results.evidence: agents/qa.md:412-414 (files_modified only); agents/changelog-analyst.md:125 (files_modified and files_created); ticket M4 vs M5 diff form mismatch

ba_validation_objections.finding: BA spec M5 mandates: 'paths in dev.files_modified / dev.files_created that are absent from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD (at commit time, HEAD is updated)'. But agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2 runs BEFORE staging and commit (Phase 2 = file classification, Phase 5 = staging, Phase 6+ = commit). At Phase 2, HEAD is NOT yet updated, so ..HEAD would return an empty diff set and falsely classify all legitimate uncommitted changes as provenance_anomaly. The implementation correctly uses the working-tree form (git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>) without ..HEAD. The BA spec M5 text is internally inconsistent with the Edge Cases section (ticket line 117) which correctly says changelog-analyst runs pre-commit. The literal M5 text ('at commit time, HEAD is updated') is factually wrong for Phase 2. BA MUST update M5 to specify the working-tree form (no ..HEAD) to match the correct implementation.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA updates M5 to: 'paths absent from git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working-tree diff — changelog-analyst Phase 2 runs before commit so HEAD is not yet updated; ..HEAD form would return empty set). AND not in baseline_dirty_snapshot are classified as provenance_anomaly'. Remove the erroneous 'at commit time, HEAD is updated' parenthetical from M5.

ba_validation_objections.finding: The AC4 test checks for 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' OR 'diff --name-only' in Phase 2 text. This accepts the wrong ..HEAD form if it were present. The test does not assert the absence of '..HEAD' in the provenance filter. The test should explicitly verify the working-tree form is used and that ..HEAD is absent from the provenance filter section, to prevent a future regression that reintroduces ..HEAD.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: Update AC4 test to: (1) assert 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' appears in Phase 2 provenance filter WITHOUT '..HEAD'; (2) assert '..HEAD' does not appear in the provenance filter step 1 text.

ba_validation_objections.finding: The QA provenance check in agents/qa.md step 4 reads: 'For every path in dev.files_modified that is absent from git diff...'. The check covers only files_modified, not files_created. Yet the task context (ticket line 12: 'dev.files_modified and files_created'), changelog-analyst.md:125 (applies filter to both files_modified and files_created), and the root cause (dev claiming created files that were already committed) all require coverage of files_created too. Newly created (untracked) files do not appear in git diff --name-only output, meaning they can only be validated via git status --porcelain --short with untracked-file detection. M4 text also only says 'dev.files_modified' which creates an AC that is narrower than the requirement intent. This is not blocking the fundamental mechanism but leaves a real gap.
ba_validation_objections.proposed_fix: BA updates M4 and AC3 to: extend provenance check to also cover dev.files_created. For files_created, the validation set must include untracked files from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (since new files do not appear in git diff). A path in files_created that is neither in tracked diff nor in untracked file list nor in baseline_dirty_snapshot is a provenance_anomaly for files_created.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The core mechanism is in place: baseline is captured at dispatch, dev must derive files_modified from git diff, QA fails on inflated claims, changelog-analyst excludes anomaly paths. The provenance loop is closed for files_modified. The gaps are in scope precision (files_created) and BA spec text accuracy (M5 ..HEAD form).

provenance_check.verdict: pass — all 4 files in dev.files_modified appear in git diff vs baseline_head_sha. No provenance violations.
provenance_check.note: dev.files_created (5 test files under tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/) are untracked new files. They do not appear in git diff (expected for untracked) and were not in baseline_dirty_snapshot. The QA provenance check as written only covers files_modified, so no provenance_violation is raised. However this is the gap identified in ba_validation_objections.

Verifies agents/qa.md has dev_provenance_violation, baseline_head_sha, primary_cause dev_implementation

execution.qa_note: Test PASSES but is under-enforcing: it accepts any 'diff --name-only' text including the ..HEAD form. Test does not assert ..HEAD is absent from the provenance filter. This is a test quality gap, not a test failure.

generated_tests.description: Verifies graceful skip language when baseline_head_sha is absent in both qa.md and changelog-analyst.md

all_findings.issue: BA spec M5 mandates 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD (at commit time, HEAD is updated)' for changelog-analyst. But changelog-analyst Phase 2 (file classification) runs BEFORE staging and commit. At Phase 2, HEAD is not updated, so ..HEAD returns an empty diff and falsely flags all legitimate changes as provenance_anomaly. The implementation correctly uses working-tree form (no ..HEAD). BA spec M5 literal text is wrong and must be updated. This is spec_text_vs_execution_drift — the AC4 THEN clause says 'absent from the baseline diff' which does not specify the form, but M5 is explicit and wrong.
all_findings.recommendation: BA updates M5: replace '..HEAD (at commit time, HEAD is updated)' with '(working-tree diff — Phase 2 runs before commit; ..HEAD would return empty set)'. Also update AC4 test to assert ..HEAD is absent from the Phase 2 provenance filter text.

all_findings.issue: QA provenance check covers only dev.files_modified, not dev.files_created. The root cause incident involved dev claiming both modified AND created files already in git HEAD. Newly created untracked files cannot be validated by git diff --name-only (which only shows tracked changes). M4 and AC3 are scoped only to files_modified, leaving files_created unguarded.
all_findings.recommendation: BA extends M4 and AC3 to cover files_created. For files_created provenance: use git ls-files --others --exclude-standard to enumerate untracked files; a path in files_created absent from both the tracked diff AND the untracked list AND not in baseline_dirty_snapshot is a provenance_anomaly.

all_findings.issue: AC4 test is under-enforcing. It checks for 'diff --name-only' in Phase 2 text but does not assert that '..HEAD' is absent. A future regression reintroducing ..HEAD would pass the test. The test was updated by dev (per codex consultation) to accept the working-tree form but did not add the negative assertion for ..HEAD.
all_findings.recommendation: Update test_AC4 to: assert '..HEAD' does NOT appear in the Phase 2 provenance filter step 1 text. The check at lines 41-43 should be: assert 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' in phase2_text and '..HEAD' not in [line for line in phase2_text.split('\n') if 'provenance filter' in line.lower() or 'compute' in line.lower()].

failures.issue: spec_text_vs_execution_drift: BA spec M5 literal text mandates ..HEAD diff form for changelog-analyst but the correct and implemented form is working-tree (no ..HEAD). BA must update M5 text to match the implementation.
failures.recommendation: BA cycle required: update M5 and AC4 THEN clause to specify working-tree form. No dev change needed — the implementation is correct.

spec_section_updates.section_4:
Cycle 1 measured state (2026-05-23):
- commands/dev.md Step 8: baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot present in dispatch payload body (lines 678-679). PASS.
- agents/dev.md Output Format: git-diff derivation mandate at line 492; observed_preexisting[] at line 494 (prose) and line 516 (schema); baseline_head_sha at schema line 509; baseline_dirty_snapshot at schema line 510. PASS.
- agents/qa.md Step 5: provenance check present (lines 403-423); dev_provenance_violation label, primary_cause: dev_implementation, baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot exclusion all present. Scope: files_modified only (not files_created). PARTIAL.
- agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2: provenance_anomaly filter present (lines 117-128); uses working-tree form (no ..HEAD) — correct. Graceful skip on absent baseline present. PASS (implementation), but BA spec M5 literal text says ..HEAD (wrong).
- pytest 5/5 tests pass.
- Provenance self-check: all 4 claimed modified files confirmed in git diff vs baseline_head_sha 1a1d058.
spec_section_updates.section_6:
Gap vs acceptance criteria:
1. BA spec M5 literal text mandates '..HEAD' diff form for changelog-analyst Phase 2. Implementation correctly uses working-tree form (no ..HEAD) — the spec text is wrong. M5 says 'at commit time, HEAD is updated' but Phase 2 runs before commit/staging. This is spec_text_vs_execution_drift.
2. QA provenance check (M4/AC3) covers only dev.files_modified. The root cause incident involved both modified and created files. files_created is unguarded by the QA check.
3. AC4 test accepts any 'diff --name-only' text and does not assert ..HEAD is absent, leaving a regression path open.
spec_section_updates.section_7:
Required actions:
1. BA MUST update M5: change '..HEAD (at commit time, HEAD is updated)' to '(working-tree diff — no ..HEAD — Phase 2 runs before commit; ..HEAD would return empty set)'. Update AC4 THEN clause to explicitly specify working-tree form.
2. BA MUST extend M4 and AC3: add provenance check for dev.files_created using untracked-file detection (git ls-files --others --exclude-standard). Update AC3 test to cover files_created.
3. Dev MUST update test_AC4 to assert ..HEAD is absent from the Phase 2 provenance filter step 1 text.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex challenged my initial FAIL ground #1 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift on ..HEAD): OBSERVATION_ONLY — the working-tree form IS correct for Phase 2 (pre-commit); the BA spec M5 literal text is what is wrong. Codex provided three findings with PROPOSED_FIX: (1) AC4 test is under-enforcing — should assert ..HEAD is absent; (2) provenance check gap for files_created vs untracked files; (3) baseline_dirty_snapshot comparison is underspecified for parsing. All findings incorporated.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Revised verdict: FAIL is maintained but the primary_cause is ba_spec (M5 text wrong), not dev_implementation (implementation is correct). Three findings elevated: BA spec M5 drift (critical/ba_spec), files_created scope gap (major/ba_spec), AC4 test under-enforcement (major/dev_implementation). The ..HEAD reversal claim was reclassified from spec_text_vs_execution_drift-on-implementation to spec_text_vs_execution_drift-on-BA-spec-text.

failed_criteria: BA spec M5 literal text is wrong (says ..HEAD, implementation correctly uses working-tree form)
AC3/M4 scope is narrower than the requirement intent — files_created not covered by QA provenance check
AC4 test does not assert ..HEAD is absent from Phase 2 provenance filter
critical_issues: BA must update M5 and AC4 THEN clause to specify working-tree diff form (no ..HEAD) for changelog-analyst Phase 2. The implementation is correct; the spec text is the artifact that needs updating.
refined_context.recommended_approach: Two-track resolution: (1) BA-only fix: update ticket M5 text and AC4 THEN clause. No dev file change needed — the 4 modified agent files are correct. (2) Dev fix: update test_AC4 to assert ..HEAD absent; optionally extend QA provenance check to cover files_created using untracked-file detection.
refined_context.additional_context: Codex independently verified that changelog-analyst Phase 2 (file classification) runs before Phase 5 (staging) and any commit. The working-tree diff form is correct at Phase 2. The BA spec M5 parenthetical 'at commit time, HEAD is updated' is factually wrong for Phase 2's execution timing.
